title: grindelia pulchella var. pulchella
intro: Grindelia pulchella var. pulchella, also known as gumweed, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the western United States and Canada. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of this plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Grindelia pulchella var. pulchella include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and soothing sore throats, as well as being used to treat eczema, acne, and other skin irritations. Grindelia pulchella var. pulchella can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or poultice to utilize its medicinal properties, making it a valuable herb in natural and holistic medicine.
